title: HK falls, and US may be the next.

replies.msg:
It’s okay for Americans to not care about Hong Kong. Who would care about a place miles away, fighting for who knows what?

Really, it doesn’t matter what happened in HK.

What matter is what may soon happen in America, what may happen to Americans, to you , to your friends and family.

Daryl Morey, who merely tweeted a tweet supporting HK and its fight for freedom, are suffering fierce attacks from China. Thousands of angry Chinese replied NMSL (which refers to a Chinese curse of wishing your mother death) and other offensive curse words. Tencent announced they will no long broadcast any NBA matches if it involves the Rocket. The official newspaper of China, people’s daily, released an opinion article, literally demanded an apology from Morey for his speech. The Chinese Basketball Association (CBA) decided to sever all ties with the Rockets.

Man, that is a really costly tweet.

So at last, the NBA made a statement saying they recognised that Morey’s comment had anger a lot of Chinese fans, which is “regrettable”. Rocket’s team owner, Tilman Fertitta went on Twitter saying Morey’s view didn’t not represent Rocket in any way.

Morey eventually deleted his tweet, before adding that he now had “considered other perspectives”. He went on saying he didn’t intend to offend Chinese fans.

You may have more or less probably heard about this. If not, then you do now. But then, what does Morey have to do with anything?

The answer is, you may be the next Morey.

Just because he said something China didn’t like, Morey, and Rocket, and NBA, all have to be blasted with hateful comments and threats, put under pressure to apologize, forced into silence in order to exchange financial gains.

It could be you the next. What if you say something China doesn’t like?

It could be a causal comments saying you dislike some Chinese stuff, a retweet of China meme, a funny joke involving Xi, or just a like from you on a sarcastic post mocking China.

The possibility is endless. The warning from China, demonstrated so delicately by the MoreY incident, is never clearer.

You either stay slicened, or sides with China, or you will be threatened, attacked and punished.

You company may choose to fire you under Chinese pressure. Your superior may have to issue a statement claiming your view doesn’t represent the stance of the company. You colleagues may also be overwhelmed by NMSL comments.

All of these because you exercise your freedom of speech. Is that not clear what China is trying to do?

China wants everyone to kowtow to it. It can not tolerate any negative comments and criticism. To achieve this, it will try it best to prosecute those who dare to speak up in every possible ways. China can use the allowance of entrance to its market to blackmail, hire internet bots to attack, and put pressure to silence you.

There could be no freedom of speech, or any form of freedom under Communism.

This is what happening at HK now. And if no one stops China, US will be the next.

What happent to Morey will happen to more and more. One day, it will be you.

Wake up Americans. Fight China and protect freedom before it is too late.
Can't agree more
